strategies.name: Focused Function Patch
strategies.approach: Enhance the isValidKey function to also exclude symbol keys and keys that are inherited or non-enumerable, and update the mixinDeep function to use Object.keys() instead of for..in to avoid enumerating prototype properties and symbols. Further, validate keys using Object.hasOwnProperty to confirm own properties only, and prevent prototype pollution through all keys by filtering keys explicitly and recursively applying these validations in the mixin function.

reasoning: Prototype pollution vulnerabilities often arise from untrusted keys inherited through the prototype chain or symbol keys that can be used to manipulate Object.prototype or other internal JS objects. The original code only filtered three string keys in isValidKey and iterated with for..in which enumerates enumerable properties in the prototype chain including potential symbol keys. Switching to Object.keys() limits to own enumerable string keys, while ignoring symbols. Extending isValidKey to reject symbols and confirming keys as own properties prevents indirect contamination. These combined changes broadly secure the merge process from prototype pollution at every nested level.
